commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Fir-trees. Some take these to be another species of cedars, as they say fir is too slender and corruptible; (Martin) and Solomon had not asked for it, ver. 6.; though he does in 2 Paralipomenon ii. 8, where (Haydock) the word is translated archeuthina, "juniper-trees "by the Septuagint and St. Jerome. (Calmet)
Beroshim, is rendered fir-trees by Pagnin; box or cedars, by others. The precise import of the Hebrew names of plants, animals, is not sufficiently known. (Menochius)
Fir is use by the best architects. (Virtuvius, ii. 9.) (Calmet)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
